data.title: Hasbro outlines $1B cost-saving target by 2027 with focus on digital growth and partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Chris Cocks highlighted Hasbro's strong performance in 2024, with the Wizards of the Coast and Digital Games segment achieving record operating margins exceeding 40%. Licensing also saw significant growth, driven by partnerships and over 4,000 collaborations.
  • Key product successes included MAGIC: THE GATHERING, Monopoly Go!, and Baldur’s Gate 3, with MAGIC showing strong engagement and record demand for several tentpole sets, including its Marvel-themed Secret Lair.
  • Consumer Products returned to profitability with improved inventory management and reduced discounting. Notable product momentum was seen in Beyblade, Transformers, and Marvel's preschool range.
  • Hasbro unveiled its "Playing to Win" strategy, focused on five pillars: aging up play experiences, digital leadership, audience expansion, partnerships, and profitable franchises.
  • Cocks announced an increased cost-saving target of $1 billion by 2027, up from $750 million, with 50% expected to impact the bottom line.
  • CFO Gina Goetter mentioned delivering $227 million in net cost savings during 2024, contributing to a record operating margin.

Outlook

  • Hasbro expects total 2025 revenue to grow slightly on a constant currency basis. Wizards revenue is forecasted to grow 5%-7%, supported by major MAGIC: THE GATHERING releases like Final Fantasy, Spider-Man, and a yet-to-be-disclosed set.
  • Consumer Products revenue is projected to be flat to down 4%, with closeout volume stabilizing year-over-year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2025 is $1.1 billion to $1.15 billion, driven by continued Consumer Products profitability improvement.
  • Longer-term, Hasbro targets mid-single-digit revenue CAGR through 2027, supported by digital gaming launches, including 2026's self-published video game Exodus, and MAGIC expansions.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue totaled $1.1 billion, with adjusted operating profit of $113 million for a margin of 10.2%. Adjusted EPS was $0.46.
  • Full-year 2024 revenue reached $4.1 billion, with adjusted operating profit of $838 million and adjusted EPS of $4.01.
  • Wizards revenue grew 4% for the year but declined 7% in Q4 due to fewer set releases. Monopoly Go! generated $38 million in Q4 revenue.
  • Consumer Products revenue declined 12% in 2024 but achieved a 6% adjusted operating margin, up 6.7 points year-over-year.
  • Operating cash flow improved to $847 million, with $695 million in cash on the balance sheet at year-end.

Q&A

  • Megan Clapp, Morgan Stanley: Asked about Consumer Products performance excluding NERF and Star Wars. CEO Cocks noted growth in sub-$10 and sub-$20 segments, with MAGIC and licensing also contributing.
  • Christopher Horvers, JPMorgan Chase: Questioned the outlook for MAGIC releases. Cocks highlighted strong demand for Final Fantasy and Spider-Man sets, with potential to attract new players.
  • Alex Perry, Bank of America: Asked about Monopoly Go! and digital gaming strength. Cocks emphasized sustained engagement and marketing effectiveness, with Baldur’s Gate 3 doubling revenue expectations.
  • Kylie Cohu, Jefferies: Inquired about medium-term margin expansion drivers. CFO Goetter identified toys’ profitability and ongoing cost savings as significant contributors.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on MAGIC’s growth prospects and the potential of digital games. Concerns centered on Consumer Products headwinds, including structural challenges in NERF and Star Wars.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly around MAGIC and cost-saving initiatives. However, they acknowledged ongoing challenges in Consumer Products and the impact of set timing on Wizards margins.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a consistent level of scrutiny, while management’s tone reflected increased confidence in long-term strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted to highlight a $1 billion cost-saving target by 2027, up from $750 million.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward digital gaming partnerships and self-publishing, including the 2026 release of Exodus.
  • MAGIC’s growth trajectory remains a focal point, with major 2025 releases expected to drive engagement.
  • Analysts continued to prioritize Wizards and Consumer Products performance, with concerns around revenue sustainability and structural challenges.
  • Management’s tone grew more confident, emphasizing operational improvements and cost savings.

Risks and Concerns

  • Structural category declines in NERF and softness in Star Wars remain challenges.
  • The timing of MAGIC set releases and reliance on licensing revenue pose potential risks.
  • Tariffs on imports from China and other geopolitical factors could impact supply chain costs.
  • Analysts raised concerns about market share performance and execution risks in Consumer Products.

Final Takeaway

Hasbro closed 2024 with record operating margins and significant progress in its transformation strategy. MAGIC: THE GATHERING continues to be a key growth driver, supported by strong licensing performance and digital gaming initiatives. The "Playing to Win" strategy marks a pivot toward multiyear growth, emphasizing partnerships, cost savings, and digital innovation. Challenges remain in Consumer Products, but management’s confidence in achieving $1 billion in cost savings by 2027 and driving mid-single-digit revenue growth positions Hasbro for long-term success.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Adidas cozies up to Amazon by joining the Buy with Prime service
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) announced on Thursday the expansion of its Buy with Prime service to include Adidas' (OTCQX:ADDYY) website and app.

The new partnership will begin in the spring. During checkout for Adidas (ADDY)) customers, shoppers will see an option to log into their Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) account and verify their Prime membership. Once confirmed, they’ll finish checkout using one of Adidas' (OTCQX:ADDYY) various payment options. After the purchase is made, Amazon (AMZN) fulfills all Prime eligible items in the order.

"Expanding Buy with Prime to adidas.com and the adidas app is like lacing up a fresh pair of sneakers, it just feels right," stated Amazon (AMZN) VP Peter Larsen.

Currently, Nike (NKE) and Under Armour (UAA) do not participate in the Amazon (AMZN) Buy with Prime program.

On a broad scale, Amazon's (AMZN) Buy with Prime service is seen representing a significant development in e-commerce, offering substantial benefits for merchants, enhanced shopping experiences for consumers, and potentially reshaping the competitive landscape of online retail.

data.title: iQIYI prices offering of $350 million convertible senior notes due 2030
data.description:
• iQIYI (NASDAQ:IQ) on Thursday announced the pricing of its upsized offering $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.625% convertible senior notes due 2030.
  • The notes will bear interest at a rate of 4.625% per year, payable quarterly in arrears on March 15, June 15, September 15 and December 15 of each year, beginning on June 15, 2025. The notes will mature on March 15, 2030.
  • The notes offering is expected to close on or about February 24, 2025, and the net proceeds of the notes offering amounted to approximately $344.8 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds for the repayment and/or repurchase of existing debt securities as described below and general corporate purposes.
  • Shortly after the pricing of the notes, the company entered into separate and individually privately negotiated agreements with certain holders of its existing convertible senior notes to repurchase approximately $300 million principal amount of the existing notes.
  • IQ is -6.61% to $2.26.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Accenture invests in Voltron Data
data.description:
• Accenture (NYSE:ACN) said it has invested in Voltron Data.
  • Voltron Data is a company focused on building software to access the next generation of accelerated computing.
  • The investment will support Voltron Data to help organizations use advanced computing technology.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Voltron Data became the latest company to join Accenture Ventures’ Project Spotlight, which is a vertical accelerator for data and AI companies.

data.title: Leading Indicator Index trails consensus with an unexpected dip in January
data.description:
January Leading Indicator Index: -0.3% to 101.5 vs. 0.0% consensus and +0.1% prior (revised from -0.1%), according to data from The Conference Board released on Thursday.

Lagging Economic Index: 119.3. vs. 118.7 prior (revised from 118.5).

data.title: Mind Technology pulls back after reaching new 52-week high
data.description:
• Mind Technology (NASDAQ:MIND) pulled back on Thursday, sliding nearly 5.5%, after it hit a new 52-week high earlier in the week.
  • Shares have gained more than 28% year-to-date and more than 60% over the past 12 months, aided in part by stronger-than-expected results, and revenue and margin growth.
  • The Huntsville, Texas-based company, which reported third-quarter results in December, markets geophysical and other equipment to seismic data acquisition contractors.
  • It is ranked 1 out of 47 in the oil and gas sector and 15 out of 4,384, according to Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings system.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: BABA, WMT, SHAK, PLTR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged down on Thursday as investors digested the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s latest policy meeting, which highlighted concerns that Trump’s policy proposals, particularly tariffs, could reignite inflation in the U.S.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) surged over 8% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results, with a 6% rise in reported revenue and a 9% increase on an organic basis. The company’s 2025 guidance projects revenue between $9.9B and $10.15B, with a midpoint of $10.03B, exceeding the consensus estimate of $9.91B. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to range from $3.53B to $3.68B, while adjusted cash flow from operations is forecasted between $975M and $1.025B.
  • Herbalife (NYSE:HLF) shares jumped 18% after delivering strong Q4 results and announcing a leadership transition, with President Stephan Gratziani set to become CEO on May 1, 2025, while current CEO Michael Johnson will move to Executive Chairman. The company’s outlook projects Q1 net sales declining 1.5% to 5.5% Y/Y, though constant currency sales could be flat to up 4%, with adjusted EBITDA expected between $140M and $150M. For FY2025, net sales are forecasted to range from a 3% decline to a 3% increase, while constant currency sales could rise 1% to 7%, and adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $600M and $640M.
  • Chinese retailer Alibaba Group Holdings (BABA) soared 10% after strong FQ3 earnings, beating expectations across key metrics. The e-commerce giant reported an adjusted net profit of 51.07B yuan ($7.01B), up 6% Y/Y, surpassing estimates. Revenue rose 8%, and adjusted EBITA grew 4% Y/Y to $7.52B. The cloud intelligence segment expanded 13.1%, while International Digital Commerce Group revenue soared 32% Y/Y, significantly outpacing expectations.
  • Shares of Unity Software (U) gained as much as 18% after posting strong Q4 results, despite a weak Q1 revenue outlook. The company guided Q1 revenue between $405M and $415M, missing the $440.07M consensus. However, its net loss narrowed to $122.52M from $253.99M a year ago, with loss per share improving to $0.30 from $0.66.
  • Shake Shack (SHAK) added 12% after reporting strong Q4 earnings, with revenue up 14.8% and same-Shack sales rising 4.3%. Cost efficiencies improved margins, as food and paper costs fell to 28.0% of sales (vs. 29.1% a year ago), labor costs declined to 26.9% (vs. 28.5%), and occupancy expenses also decreased. Restaurant-level profit margin expanded 290 bps to 22.7%, while adjusted EBITDA jumped to $46.7M from $31.4M a year ago. The company opened 43 new company-operated Shacks and 33 licensed locations. However, Q1 revenue guidance of $326.5M to $330.9M (midpoint $328.7M) fell short of the $332.7M consensus.

Biggest stock losers

  • Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) fell over 5% after a memo from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, obtained by the Washington Post, revealed plans for an 8% annual cut to the defense budget over the next five years. The proposed reductions, due by Feb. 24, raised concerns about potential impacts on defense contracts. However, 17 categories are reportedly exempt, including border security, nuclear weapons modernization, missile defense, and one-way attack drones.
  • Shares of Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) dropped around 10% despite a strong Q4 performance, which saw the company beat top- and bottom-line estimates, increase car sales, and improve profit per vehicle. While Carvana expects “significant growth” in retail units sold and adjusted EBITDA in FY2025, with a sequential increase in both metrics in Q1, investor concerns may have been driven by its Form S-3 filing with the SEC, allowing for the potential sale of an undisclosed number of shares. The company’s long-term targets include gross profit growth of 15%-19% and an EBITDA margin of 8%-13.5%.
  • Retail giant Walmart (WMT) saw its shares tumble 7% as its profit and sales outlook fell short of estimates, despite exceeding Q4 expectations. For 1QFY26, the company expects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), below the 3.86% consensus, while adjusted EPS is forecasted between $0.57 and $0.58 (midpoint $0.575), missing the $0.65 consensus. For FY2026, Walmart projects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), trailing the 4.19% consensus, with adjusted EPS guidance of $2.50 to $2.60 (midpoint $2.55), compared to the $2.77 consensus. The weaker-than-expected outlook highlights ongoing competitive pressures and potential margin constraints.
  • Despite beating Q4 estimates, shares of Nice (NICE) plunged 12% as its Q1 and full-year 2025 outlook missed expectations. The cloud solutions provider projected Q1 revenue between $693M and $703M, with a midpoint of $698M, falling short of the $725.52M consensus. Non-GAAP EPS is expected between $2.78 and $2.88, with a midpoint of $2.83, below the $2.90 consensus. For FY2025, Nice forecasts revenue between $2.918B and $2.938B, with a midpoint of $2.928B, missing the $3.00B consensus. The company also guided full-year EPS between $12.13 and $12.33, with a midpoint of $12.23, trailing the $12.32 consensus. The outlook includes a projected 12% Y/Y increase in cloud revenue.

data.title: These are the countries that could be most affected by new tariffs on autos
data.description:
New tariffs on pharmaceuticals, semis, and autos could affect more disproportionately imports from nations with a higher imbalance in trade, including Mexico, Japan, South Korea, and Europe.

President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced new "25% or higher" tariffs on imports of pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, and autos, and that he would reveal more about it on April 2.

The U.S. motor vehicles trade deficit is more imbalanced with Mexico, at almost $100B in imports vs. $5B in exports in value, according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.

U.S. motor vehicles trade deficit with Japan is also imbalanced at $40B in imports vs. very low exports.

Canada, on the other hand, is the most balanced trade, slightly skewed towards more imports than exports in auto vehicles.

China is the only major trade partner where exports are slightly higher than imports.

Auto ETFs: (NASDAQ:DRIV), (NYSEARCA:KARS), (NASDAQ:CARZ), (NYSEARCA:EVAV), (CARU), (CARD)

Country ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWW), (NYSEARCA:EWJ), (NYSEARCA:EWY), (NYSEARCA:VGK), (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NASDAQ:MCHI)

Big motor vehicle companies: (F), (TM), (HMC), (TSLA), (GM), (RIVN), (STLA), (LCID)

data.title: OpenAI crosses 400M users for ChatGPT amid DeepSeek challenge
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI had 400 million weekly active users for ChatGPT as of February.

It is a 33% increase from the 300 million weekly active users as of Dec. 4, 2024, an OpenAI spokesperson said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The company had over 2 million paying business users, compared to 1 million as of Sept. 5, the spokesperson added.

OpenAI noted that developer traffic was up two times in the last six months, and five times for its reasoning models since the launch of the o3 model.

“There’s an overall effect of people really wanting these tools, and seeing that these tools are really valuable," said OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap in an interview with CNBC.

OpenAI counts Uber, Morgan Stanley, Moderna and T-Mobile among some of its largest enterprise customers, the report added.

While the consumer business may grow faster as people can adopt it at will, the enterprise is in the “process of building up,” Lightcap noted, according to the report.

“There’s a buying cycle there, and a learning process that goes into scaling an enterprise business,” said Lightcap. “AI is going to be like cloud services. It’s going to be something that you can’t run a business that ultimately is not really running on these very powerful models underneath the surface,” added Lightcap, as per the report.

Apple's (AAPL) integration of OpenAI's AI model started in December last year. Apple Intelligence relies on a mix of Apple’s own AI technology and a collaboration with OpenAI.

OpenAI’s growth comes amid competition from Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK). Tech stocks plunged on Jan. 27, after DeepSeek last month unveiled its AI model R1 and detailed in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision. Nvidia (NVDA) saw around $589B in market cap erased on Jan. 27.

“DeepSeek is a testament to how much AI is like entered the public consciousness in the mainstream -- it would have been unfathomable two years ago,” said Lightcap. “It’s a moment that shows how powerful these models are and how much people really care.”

Earlier this week, several executives noted that DeepSeek does not pose any meaningful threat to the businesses of AI companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic, which is backed by Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN).

Last week, Google's AI head, reportedly, told employees that he was not worried about China’s DeepSeek AI and noted that the U.S. company has superior AI technology,

Lightcap also commented on the unsolicited $97.4B offer by a group led by Elon Musk, which was rejected by OpenAI.

“The numbers tell the story,” Lightcap noted. “We try to be very transparent about where we stand on all of this. [Musk] is a competitor. He’s competing. It’s an unorthodox way of competing, the report added.”
